summary: quizzl uses pure React Context + idb-keyval with no Zustand, no service worker, and no Web Workers. MarmotClient is constructed in MarmotContext.tsx with signer/groupStateBackend/keyPackageStore/network params; a `media` factory is the obvious extension point. The signer path is useNostrIdentity().privateKeyHex -> createPrivateKeySigner (signerAdapter.ts:11), already used by BackupContext for signing relay events — same pattern fits kind 24242 Blossom auth events.

key_files.purpose: MarmotClient construction, storage factory wiring point
leaveGroup() cleanup hooks - where clearMediaRefs goes
Optimistic send pattern with crypto.randomUUID() tempId, no reconciliation
Per-domain IDB store declarations and Backend classes
Per-group IDB store pattern, appendMessage queue, clearMessages
output:'export', trailingSlash, swcMinify:false (Terser), no worker config
Context nesting; dynamic({ssr:false}) wrappers for crypto providers
Constants-only config module pattern to mirror for config/blossom.ts

module_layout.components: Flat root (Layout, ProfileSummary, etc.) plus components/groups/ (16 files, 50-416 lines, modals colocated) and components/quiz/. PascalCase, one component per file.
module_layout.context: Six providers: LanguageContext, ProfileContext, BackupContext, NostrIdentityContext, MarmotContext, ChatStoreContext, PollStoreContext. First four are app-wide; ChatStoreProvider+PollStoreProvider are mounted per-group-detail in pages/groups.tsx:258-282.
module_layout.hooks: Six existing: useMoodTheme, useOnlineStatus, useSelectedTopics, useStudyTimer, useThemeStyles, useTopicProgress. useXxx naming.
module_layout.lib: Domain modules at root (ndkClient, storage, nostrKeys, linkify, i18n, scoring, etc.); lib/marmot/ for group logic (12 files). NO lib/media/ or lib/files/ — clean slate for the feature.
module_layout.config: Only config/profile.ts (constants only). New config/blossom.ts follows same pattern.

static_export_constraints: next.config.mjs:3 sets output:'export', trailingSlash:true, swcMinify:false (Terser, kept for ts-mls/marmot-ts bitwise ops). NO next/image anywhere — all images use Chakra UI which renders as plain . Blob URLs work in static export. Image lightbox + bubble can use Chakra directly.

marmot_group_lifecycle.constructor_signature: new MarmotClient({ signer, groupStateBackend: new KeyValueGroupStateBackend(new IdbGroupStateBackend()), keyPackageStore: new KeyPackageStore(new IdbKeyPackageBackend()), network: new NdkNetworkAdapter(ndk) })
marmot_group_lifecycle.where_to_wire_media_factory: app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx:259 (immediately after IdbKeyPackageBackend instantiation, within init() scope)

ndk_signer_access.signer_construction: createPrivateKeySigner(privateKeyHex) from app/src/lib/marmot/signerAdapter.ts:11 returns applesauce-core EventSigner
ndk_signer_access.example_call_in_codebase: BackupContext.tsx:36-42 — already does exactly this pattern for signing backup events
ndk_signer_access.ndk_singleton_signer: lib/ndkClient.ts:9,29 also exposes ndkInstance.signer (NDKPrivateKeySigner) but codebase consistently prefers createPrivateKeySigner for the applesauce-core interface

web_worker_support: ZERO Web Workers used today. vitest.config.ts has no worker config. next.config.mjs has no webpack worker rules. `new Worker(new URL(..., import.meta.url))` with Next 14 + output:'export' is untested and would be NEW infrastructure. Spec-noted fallback (main-thread processing in requestIdleCallback) is recommended for v1 to avoid scope creep, with feature detection at module load.

optimistic_ui_pattern.send_path: 1) optimistic added to state immediately (line 182), 2) sendChatSafe() awaited, 3) on success: persisted to IDB via appendMessage (line 186), 4) on failure: filtered out by tempId (line 190)
optimistic_ui_pattern.important_caveat: Optimistic message is NEVER replaced by the real rumor ID. id stays = tempId in memory and IDB. Real rumor (when echoed back) would be a separate message — but the sender's own message is filtered at MarmotContext.tsx:515 (`if (senderPubkey === pubkeyHex) return`). Image send pipeline must follow same convention.

state_management: Pure React Context + useState/useRef/useCallback/useMemo. No Zustand, Redux, reducers. setMessages functional updates throughout. Re-render efficiency via useMemo at context value level (MarmotContext.tsx:1034). unreadStore.ts uses module-level variable (lightweight non-context global).

group_leave_path.note: There is no MLS group.leave() — soft-leave only. Spec text 'group.leave()' refers to this leaveGroup function.

pwa_status: NO service worker. app/public/ contains favicon.ico, robots.txt, content/ only — no sw.js, no workbox-*.js, no manifest.json. _app.tsx registers no SW. Blossom fetches will not be intercepted.
bundle_conventions: next.config.mjs has no experimental.optimizePackageImports, no bundlePages, no size budgets. Only webpack customization is a next-swc-loader passthrough for 4 packages that crash SWC. blurhash (~5KB gzipped) is fine.

established_patterns.idb_store_per_domain: createStore('quizzl-{domain}', '{domain}') at module scope in the relevant persistence file
established_patterns.version_counter_bump: MarmotContext bumps chatVersion/profileVersion/pollVersion after IDB writes; downstream contexts re-read IDB reactively
established_patterns.lazy_dynamic_import: MarmotContext.tsx:249-253 uses await import(...) inside useEffect to avoid SSR crashes; all marmot-ts and NDK imports follow this
established_patterns.soft_error_handling: every marmot-ts call in try/catch with console.warn; opts.softFail pattern for fire-and-forget paths (sendRumorSafe, MarmotContext.tsx:71-95)
established_patterns.buildRumor_helper: MarmotContext.tsx:98-109 builds MIP-03 application message rumors with getEventHash; all message kinds (score, profile, chat, poll) use this helper. Image rumor MUST use buildRumor too.

open_questions: Does buildRumor accept arbitrary tags or does it only set kind+content+pubkey+created_at? Architect must verify before implementing the imeta tags path.
Does the existing MarmotClient construction expect a `media` factory at v0.4.0 or a different parameter name? Verify against marmot-ts/dist/client/marmot-client.d.ts before wiring.
Will Next 14 dev mode show CORS issues against blossom.band from localhost? May need to test early.
Should clearMediaRefs run async-fire-and-forget (current convention) or block leaveGroup completion?
